name: Blue Ribbon Carrot Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a bowl, mix flour, sodium bicarbonate, salt, and ground cinnamon.
3. In another bowl, beat eggs, vegetable oil, buttermilk, sugar, and vanilla extract until smooth.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, stirring in crushed pineapple, shredded coconut, and chopped walnuts.
5. Pour batter into two greased and floured 9-inch round cake pans.
6. Bake for 35-40 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
7. Remove cakes from the oven and let cool completely on wire racks.
8. For buttermilk glaze, combine sugar, sodium bicarbonate, buttermilk, butter, light corn syrup, and vanilla extract in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 4 minutes, stirring often. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla. Allow to cool slightly before spreading over the layers.
9. For orange cream cheese frosting, beat together softened butter and cream cheese until smooth. Add vanilla, powdered sugar, grated orange rind, and juice; beat until well combined.
10. Spread buttermilk glaze evenly over the cooled cake layers.
11. Frost the top and sides of the cake with the orange cream cheese frosting.
12. Store the finished cake in the refrigerator.
Buttermilk Glaze Yield: About 1 1/2 cups
Orange Cream Cheese Frosting Yield: Enough for one 2-layer cake
